titleStmt.title: Letter from S.M.P to F. Ormsby Cooke, 23 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: S.M.P.

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from S.M.P. to Ormsby Cooke regarding the arrest of Daniel McCarthy and his transfer to Knutsford.

correspAction.placeName: Hill Publishing Company, 6-8 Bouverie Street, London, England

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
23rd June, 1916 Dear Sir, With reference to your letters of the 14th and
22nd instant, I beg to inform you that from enquiries which
I have made, I find that Daniel McCarthy was arrested under
martial law at Dunmanway on the 13th ultimo. He was sent
on to Cork, thence to Dublin and after a few days to
Knutsford, England, where he has been up to the present. His family have been in communication with him
all the time and were never at a loss to know his address. Yours truly, (Sd) S.M.P F. Ormsby Cooke, Esq., Hill Publishing Company, Ltd., 6 â 8 Bouverie Street, K. C.
